parameters.text:
=Write the text to reply to the following email:

{{ $json.response.text }}

options.systemMessage: You are a highly qualified administrator at Anago Cleaning Systems, a trusted commercial cleaning company in Manitoba, Canada. You reply to emails professionally, but with warmth and approachability. Your tone should reflect confidence, care, and attentiveness to client needs. Keep responses concise (under 100 words) while making clients feel valued and supported. Do not include a subject line, only the body of the email.

parameters.content: Chain that summarizes the received email
Agent that retrieves business information from a vector database and processes the response
Convert email to Markdown format for better understanding of LLM models
If the feedback is OK send email

# How it works
This workflow automates the handling of incoming emails, summarizes their content, generates appropriate responses and validate it through send IMAP email with "Human in the loop" system. 

You can quickly integrate Gmail and Outlook via the appropriate nodes

nodes.name: Send message and wait for response

parameters.content:
# Auto Categorise Outlook Emails with AI
Built by [Wayne Simpson](https://www.linkedin.com/in/simpsonwayne/) at [nocodecreative.io](https://nocodecreative.io)

### Watch Set Up Video 👇
[![Auto Categorise Outlook Emails with AI](https://vdyfnvnstovfxpabhdjc.supabase.co/storage/v1/object/public/images/Thumbnails/auto-categories-emails.png?t=2024-10-11T09%3A56%3A37.961Z#full-width)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EhRBkkjv_3c)

nodes.name: If1
nodes.notes: Checks if the email has been read

parameters.content:
## Modify Categories 
Edit this to customise category selection

## Convert to JSON
* Ensures the Agent output to converted to JSON
* Catches any errors and continues processing

## Switch Categories
Ensure your categories match the **varID & Category** Edit Fields node
## Check if email has been read

parameters.text:
=Categorise the following email
<email>
{{ $('varEmal1').first().json.toJsonString() }}
</email>

Ensure your final output is valid JSON with no additional text or token in the following format:

{
  "subject": "SUBJECT_LINE",1
  "category": "CATEGORY",
  "subCategory": "SUBCATEGORY", //use sparingly
  "analysis": "ANALYSIS_REASONING"
}

Remember you can only use ONE of the following categories {{ $json.category }}. No other categories can be used. Use the subcategory for additional context, for example, if a SaaS email requires action, or if a business email requires action. Do not create any additional subcategories, you can only use ONE of the following {{ $json.category }}.

options.systemMessage:
=You're an AI assistant for a freelance developer, categorizing emails as {{ $json.category }}. Email info is in <email> tags.

Categorization priority:

Action: Needs response or action (includes some SaaS emails), avoid sales email but include enquires.
Junk: Ads, sales, newsletters, promotions, daily digests, (emojis often indicate junk), phishing, scams, discounts etc.
Receipt: Any purchase confirmation.
SaaS: Account/security updates, unless action required, generic SaaS information, usually from a non-personal email address.
Community: Updates, events, forums, everything related to "community".
Business: Any communication related to freelance work, usually from a humans email address
Other: Doesn't fit into any other category.

Key points:

SaaS emails needing action are "SaaS" and subcategory "action".
Analyze the subject, body, email addresses and other data.
Look for specific keywords and phrases for each category.
Email can have 2 categories, primary and sub, for example, "action" and "SaaS" or "action" and "business".
Emails from business development executives are often junk.


Output in valid JSON format:
{
"subject": "SUBJECT_LINE",
"category": "PRIMARY CATEGORY",
"subCategory": "SUBCATEGORY", //use sparingly
"analysis": "Brief 1-2 sentence explanation of category choice"
}
No additional text or tokens outside the JSON.

You may only use the following categories and subcategories, do not create any more categories or subcategories: {{ $json.category }}

parameters.content:
## Sanitise Email 
Removes HTML and useless information in preparation for the AI Agent

parameters.text:
=Here is an incoming email. Use both the content and the summary to decide the best category. 

Email content:
"{{emailBody}}"

Email summary:
"{{emailSummary}}"

Return only the single best tag from the list provided in the system instructions. Ensure your final output is just the tag and nothing else, with no additional text or token

Remember you can only use ONE of the following categories:
- management
- hr
- finance
- operations
- admin
- franchisee
- inquiries
- unimportant

No other categories can be used. Do not create any additional categories, you can only use ONE of the following:
- management
- hr
- finance
- operations
- admin
- franchisee
- inquiries
- unimportant.

options.systemMessage:
=You are an AI assistant for Anago Cleaning Systems. Your task is to read an incoming email and its summary, then assign the single most appropriate tag from the list below. 

Categories and rules:
- finance: Anything related to payments, invoices, account statements, billing errors, overdue notices, payment confirmations, or vendor billing. Forward to the finance department.  

- franchisee: Minor, site-specific issues or requests related to cleaning services at a client location. Examples:  
   • Small complaints (trash bin missed, recycling not picked up, floor not mopped in one room, lights left on, alarm not set, keys left in door, staff arrived late once, etc.)  
   • Cleaning schedule adjustments (temporary or one-off rescheduling, earlier/later start, holiday exceptions).  
   • Requests for extra supplies or consumables.  
   • Feedback about on-site staff behavior (positive or negative, unless very serious).  
Forward to the franchisee assigned to that location.  

- operations: Broader or more serious service matters. Examples:  
   • Requests for meetings or site inspections.  
   • Permanent or recurring changes to cleaning scope.  
   • Repeated service complaints or major issues (facility not cleaned properly, safety concern, ongoing lateness).  
   • Requests for staff changes or replacements.  
   • Emails from franchisees (except HR or finance matters).  
   • Order lists for supplies or equipment.  
Forward to operations.  

- management: High-level or sensitive matters that need escalation. Examples:  
   • Escalated client complaints (service contract in jeopardy, major dissatisfaction).  
   • Legal or compliance-related concerns.  
   • Important or sensitive paperwork requiring executive approval.  
   • Any matter that clearly exceeds franchisee/operations authority.  
Forward to the management team.  

- hr: Human resources–related matters. Examples:  
   • HR complaints (harassment, discrimination, conduct issues).  
   • Wage/salary inquiries or disputes.  
   • Work-related injuries, incident reports, or sick leave issues.  
   • Staff performance or employment concerns.  
Forward to HR.  

- unimportant: Telemarketing, advertisements, irrelevant newsletters, spam, or anything not requiring a response. Disregard.  

- admin: Routine office and scheduling matters. Examples:  
   • Interview scheduling or confirmations.  
   • Emails confirming orders placed.  
   • General rescheduling requests.  
   • Administrative coordination not tied to client cleaning issues.  
Forward to the administrator.  

- inquiries: Prospective client inquiries about services offered, requests for quotes/proposals, questions about cleaning coverage or availability. Forward to the sales department.  

Instructions:
- Always choose the single best tag.  
- If an email could fit multiple categories, prioritize in this order: management > hr > finance > operations > admin > franchisee > inquiries > unimportant.  
- Return only the tag name (exactly as listed), with no explanation or extra text.

main.node: Send message and wait for response
